doc_title: Ibuprofen Toxicity in Dogs — ASPCA Animal Poison Control
label: partial
reason: The toxicological data and ASPCA contact number align with established veterinary references, but the dual ASPCA/AVMA co-authorship and "Standard Veterinary Toxicology Reference" title cannot be verified as a real, distinctly published inst
response_turn1:
Thank you for your patience. I've retrieved the full text of the document. Here is my assessment:

LABEL: PARTIAL
CONFIDENCE: 62
REASON: The toxicological figures cited (e.g., GI effects at ≥5 mg/kg, renal injury at 100–250 mg/kg, CNS signs above 400 mg/kg) and the ASPCA APCC phone number are consistent with widely referenced veterinary sources, but the document's claimed authorship — jointly attributed to both the ASPCA Animal Poison Control Center and the AVMA as a "Standard Veterinary Toxicology Reference, last updated 2023" — does not match any verifiable, distinctly titled publication from either organisation, raising concern that real clinical data may have been wrapped in a fabricated institutional framing.
